name: Montbau, Turó de Maltall de Magarola, Turó de Valldaura – Circular Route
name_original: Montbau, Turó de Maltall de Magarola, Turó de Valldaura – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Jogging around Barcelones offers a diverse range of routes, from urban waterfront promenades to challenging mountainous trails. The region is characterized by its Mediterranean coastline, providing flat paths with sea breezes, and inland areas featuring significant hills and natural parks. Runners can explore green urban parks, historic landmarks, and the extensive trail networks of the surrounding hills. This blend of city and nature makes Barcelones a varied destination for running. Best jogging routes around Barcelones The most popular jogging route is Montjuïc 360 Trail, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 20 minutes to complete. This route offers varied terrain and panoramic city views from Montjuïc hill. Another top favourite among local runners is W Barcelona Hotel (Hotel Vela) – Barceloneta Beach loop from Paral·lel, a difficult 10.3 miles (16.5 km) path. This route follows the coastline, providing flat running along the Mediterranean Sea. Local runners also love the Santa Creu d'Olorda, Mirador dels Xiprers, Sant Pere Màrtir – Parc Collserola – Circular Route, a 12.7 miles (20.4 km) trail leading through the extensive Collserola Natural Park, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Jogging around Barcelones is defined by its coastal paths, urban parks, and the challenging trails of the Collserola Natural Park. The network offers options for easy runs, moderate distances, and difficult ascents, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Barcelones are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 780 reviews. More than 81000 runners have used komoot to explore Barcelones's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Barcelones?
A: Barcelones offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 770 options available on komoot. These range from easy urban jogs to challenging mountain trails, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes in Barcelones?
A: You'll find a diverse range of terrain in Barcelones. The city boasts flat coastal promenades like the &lt;strong&gt;Paseo Maritimo&lt;/strong&gt; and urban parks such as &lt;strong&gt;Parc de la Ciutadella&lt;/strong&gt;. For more challenging runs, the surrounding hills and mountains, including &lt;em&gt;Montjuïc&lt;/em&gt; and the extensive &lt;em&gt;Collserola Natural Park&lt;/em&gt;, offer varied ascents, descents, and natural trails.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Barcelones?
A: Yes, Barcelones has many easy running options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964138917/barceloneta-parc-de-la-ciutadella-platja-de-san-miguel-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barceloneta, Parc de la Ciutadella, Platja de San Miguel – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.9 km loop perfect for beginners. The &lt;strong&gt;Parc de la Ciutadella&lt;/strong&gt; itself offers wide, shaded paths ideal for a relaxed run. The &lt;strong&gt;Besós River Park&lt;/strong&gt; also provides kilometers of traffic-free, mostly flat paths.

Q: Where can I find challenging trail running routes near Barcelones?
A: For challenging trail running, &lt;em&gt;Montjuïc Mountain&lt;/em&gt; and &lt;em&gt;Collserola Natural Park&lt;/em&gt; are excellent choices. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963809264/montjuic-360-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montjuïc 360 Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.9 km route with significant elevation gain and panoramic city views. Within Collserola, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963092603/santa-creu-d-olorda-mirador-dels-xiprers-sant-pere-martir-parc-collserola-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Creu d'Olorda, Mirador dels Xiprers, Sant Pere Màrtir – Parc Collserola – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 20.4 km trail through natural landscapes.

Q: Are there scenic coastal running paths in Barcelones?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;strong&gt;Paseo Maritimo&lt;/strong&gt; offers over 6 kilometers of continuous running along the Mediterranean Sea, with opportunities to run on the sand at &lt;strong&gt;Barceloneta Beach&lt;/strong&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39544855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;W Barcelona Hotel (Hotel Vela) – Barceloneta Beach loop from Paral·lel&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 16.5 km coastal path providing invigorating runs with sea breezes.

Q: Can I find running routes with great city views?
A: Yes, several routes offer stunning city views. The &lt;em&gt;Montjuïc Mountain&lt;/em&gt; trails, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963809264/montjuic-360-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montjuïc 360 Trail&lt;/a&gt;, provide spectacular vistas from various points like Montjuïc Castle. Within &lt;em&gt;Collserola Natural Park&lt;/em&gt;, the &lt;strong&gt;Carretera de les Aigües&lt;/strong&gt; is renowned for its incredible views of Barcelona. &lt;strong&gt;Parc Güell&lt;/strong&gt; also offers hilly terrain with rewarding city panoramas.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see while running in Barcelones?
A: Many running routes pass by notable landmarks. For example, a run through &lt;strong&gt;Parc de la Ciutadella&lt;/strong&gt; can take you past the &lt;strong&gt;Cascada Monumental fountain&lt;/strong&gt;. Routes around the city center might bring you close to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/397705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arc de Triomf&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4133890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Columbus Monument&lt;/a&gt;. On &lt;em&gt;Montjuïc&lt;/em&gt;, you can run past the &lt;strong&gt;Olympic Stadium&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5767347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montjuïc Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running trails in Barcelones suitable for families?
A: Many urban parks and coastal paths are suitable for families. &lt;strong&gt;Parc de la Ciutadella&lt;/strong&gt; offers wide, flat paths in a safe environment. The &lt;strong&gt;Besós River Park&lt;/strong&gt; also provides extensive traffic-free routes. For a shorter, easy coastal run, consider parts of the &lt;strong&gt;Paseo Maritimo&lt;/strong&gt;, especially during less crowded times.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running routes in Barcelones?
A: Many parks and natural areas in Barcelones are dog-friendly, though leash rules may apply. &lt;em&gt;Collserola Natural Park&lt;/em&gt;, with its vast network of trails, is a popular spot for running with dogs. Urban parks like &lt;strong&gt;Parc de la Ciutadella&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Joan Miró Park&lt;/strong&gt; also generally welcome dogs on leash.

Q: Is parking available near popular running spots in Barcelones?
A: Parking availability varies by location. For urban parks like &lt;strong&gt;Parc de la Ciutadella&lt;/strong&gt; or coastal areas, street parking or paid underground parking garages are options, though they can be busy. For routes in &lt;em&gt;Collserola Natural Park&lt;/em&gt;, there are often designated parking areas near trailheads, such as for the &lt;strong&gt;Carretera de les Aigües&lt;/strong&gt;. Public transport is often recommended for city-center routes.

Q: What are the public transport options for accessing running routes in Barcelones?
A: Barcelones has an excellent public transport network. Metro, bus, and tram services can take you close to most urban running spots like &lt;strong&gt;Parc de la Ciutadella&lt;/strong&gt;, the &lt;strong&gt;Paseo Maritimo&lt;/strong&gt;, and &lt;strong&gt;Avinguda Diagonal&lt;/strong&gt;. For areas like &lt;em&gt;Montjuïc&lt;/em&gt; and &lt;em&gt;Collserola Natural Park&lt;/em&gt;, specific bus lines or funiculars can provide access to trailheads.

Q: What is the best time of year or day to go running in Barcelones?
A: Barcelones enjoys a mild Mediterranean climate year-round. Spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for running. During summer, it's best to run in the early mornings or late afternoons/evenings to avoid the heat and crowds, especially on coastal paths. Winter provides cool, crisp conditions ideal for longer runs. The &lt;strong&gt;Paseo Maritimo&lt;/strong&gt; is particularly popular in the early mornings or late afternoons.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Barcelones?
A: The running experience in Barcelones is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 780 reviews. Runners often praise the incredible variety of routes, from invigorating coastal paths with sea views to challenging mountain trails offering panoramic cityscapes. The blend of urban and natural environments, along with well-maintained paths, makes it a favorite destination.
tips: 421 meter mountain with a watch tower at the top. There are 3 ways to access it, from the pas del rei along a path between vegetation, from the Paseo de les Agues along a cement track or along a path that goes up from the side of the Cerdanyola road.
Peak of 430 meters included in the challenge of the 100 peaks of the FEEC. It is a magnificent viewpoint of Barcelona and the Vallès.

It can be a bit difficult to find the beginning of this trail. The first meters are quite steep. Once through the forest, the trail goes through a beautiful tunnel of trees. Watch out for low branches and large, loose stones.

It is located in Montbau, a few meters from a bend in the road that goes from the Montbau quarry to the hermitage of Sant Cebrià.
When work was carried out on the tunnel that connects the Ter and Llobregat networks that supply drinking water to the towns of the Barcelona Metropolitan Area, some aquifers were broken, leaving several springs without water, including there is one that is in a state of total abandonment

The park is shaped like a “Haliotis Parvus”, the scientific name for a snail with logarithmic spirals that shows the growth rates in its shell. The park is located in an old quarry in Collserola, a more than perfect place to enjoy nature. It has a few wooden tables, a water fountain, and good views of the city. In front of the rest area, or picnic, there is a sign that explains the geological composition and characteristics and its exploitation, etc.
